name: Dumpling Home
review: I got my own water straw cup / plastic utensils to play with but I preferred dropping the big person utensils on the floor. I ate 2.23 dumplings and then ignored my veggie bao even though it was perfect for BLW.

name: My Father’s Kitchen
review: High chair too big for tiny me and I almost slipped out through the leg opening lmao. Good thing it's right by a children's hospital. It was worth it tho for the coconut chicken!

name: Yuanbao Jiaozi
review: My favorite dumplings in SF! I especially like smushing the fish and pepper ones. Mom bites a hole in them so they cool down for me faster. It's quiet during weekday lunch until I show up!

name: Ebisu
review: I enjoy (ruining) the cute romantic vibe at this restaurant. Mom gave me pieces of tofu from her miso soup, tamago/shrimp from a chirashi bowl, and flakes of miso cod (my fave). She did not check for high chairs as I was too small at the time.

name: Bella Trattoria
review: High chairs a bit too big for 9-month old me but seemed safe. Gnocchi was quite salty and veggies were not quite soft enough for me - but I could tell my parents loved the pasta! As for me I loved squishing the free bread and occasionally eating 2% of it before dropping it on the floor.

name: Harbin Hot Springs
review: My Inglesina chair clips to their outdoor picnic tables so I can drop as much food as I want on the ground for the birds to pick up. All their food seems super healthy and low sodium! I loved their tofu and broccoli sides. The chef even made me a baby 1-egg omelet for breakfast after I gave him very clear instructions on how I like it!
